newClaims.quote:
Health sciences education
140+ programs, internships, and rotations in 50 health sciences fields
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic College of Medicine & Science enumerates 140+ health sciences programs, internships, and rotations across 50 health sciences fields.
newClaims.limitations: System/college-wide; not narrowed to medical student visiting electives or campus.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Health-sciences internships/rotations belong to pre-residency tier although not narrowly medical-student-visiting.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 focused on the visiting medical student bullet and did not separately emit the adjacent Health sciences education programs/internships/rotations enumeration.

newClaims.quote:
Biomedical research training
Ph.D. and master's degrees in biomedical sciences
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic College of Medicine & Science offers Ph.D. and master's degrees in biomedical sciences as biomedical research training.
newClaims.limitations: Degree programs, not short research electives.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Research/graduate degree training is pre-residency tier for the biomedical pipeline.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured visiting clerkships and GME but did not enumerate the biomedical research degree pathway.

newClaims.quote: Carleigh Eagle's path to Mayo Clinic began not in an engineering lab or medical school classroom, but as an intern in the Clinical Research Internship Study Program (CRISP).
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic operates the Clinical Research Internship Study Program (CRISP), a named research internship.
newClaims.limitations: Mentioned in a news story; eligibility, application, fee not stated.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Named research internship is a Tier 1 pre-residency opportunity signal.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: Buried in a news-feed story rather than the main program enumeration; A1 stayed on the navigation section.

newClaims.quote:
Non-clinical education programs
Internships, experiences, and opportunities for learners
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic College of Medicine & Science offers non-clinical education programs comprising internships, experiences, and opportunities for learners.
newClaims.limitations: Non-clinical scope; not USCE-eligible for IMG clinical experience.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Pre-residency learner internships; explicitly non-clinical so cannot count as USCE but worth capturing.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 skipped this entry because it is labeled non-clinical and not a visiting student clerkship.

newClaims.claimText: Site links to 'Visiting medical student clerkships Admissions' destination but does not state eligibility, fee, duration, or VSLO status on this overview page.
newClaims.limitations: Overview only; admissions content not present in cleaned text.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Marker that admissions detail lives on a child page; surfaces a fetch target for next pass.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 emitted the offering but did not log the missing-field pointer to the admissions sub-page.

newClaims.quote: Residencies and fellowshipsAdmissions • Stipend and benefits
newClaims.claimText: GME residencies and fellowships section advertises Admissions plus Stipend and benefits information for trainees.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Stipend/benefits language is a Tier 2 trainee-tier financial signal A1's program-count claim did not cover.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 emitted the 350+ programs headline but skipped the adjacent Admissions/Stipend/benefits enumeration.

newClaims.quote: Mayo Clinic School of Graduate Medical Education
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic operates the Mayo Clinic School of Graduate Medical Education as the GME-administering school.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Names the GME-administering school entity; Tier 2 trainee infrastructure.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured program counts but not the formal school entity name that owns those programs.

newClaims.quote: More from Mayo ClinicPatient care and health informationResearch labs and clinical trialsGiving and philanthropyFor medical professionalsJob openings
newClaims.claimText: Site footer surfaces a 'Job openings' link to Mayo Clinic careers, a Tier 3 post-trainee signal.
newClaims.limitations: Footer link only; no role/visa detail on this page.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Job openings link is a Tier 3 attending/career signal; A1 had no Tier 3 claim.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 mined the academic body but did not record the footer Job openings Tier 3 pointer.

newClaims.quote:
Executive Education
Healthcare administration courses for leaders and medical education consulting for organizations
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic offers Executive Education: healthcare administration courses for leaders and medical education consulting for organizations.
newClaims.limitations: Healthcare administration / consulting, not clinical practice.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Executive education targets practicing leaders; Tier 3 post-trainee.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 stopped at GME and visa; did not enumerate executive-education service tier.

newClaims.quote:
Continuous professional development
Continuing education courses for medical professionals
newClaims.claimText: Mayo Clinic provides Continuous Professional Development continuing-education courses for practicing medical professionals.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: CME for practicing professionals is Tier 3 post-trainee.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 did not separate CME from the academic-program list; treated it as part of system overview.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: c1 is UNKNOWN_SCOPE but the page is the Mayo Clinic College of Medicine & Science overview covering Rochester, Phoenix/Scottsdale, Jacksonville, and Mayo Clinic Health System. Scope should be MEDICAL_SCHOOL_LEVEL and visibility must not promote PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE without campusApplicabilityProof.
f3 quote 'GME residencies and fellowships' is a weaker variant of f1's '350+ GME programs in all medical and surgical specialties'. f1 carries the program count; f3 is redundant.

f2 cites generic visa/immigration language with audience unspecified (visiting student vs trainee vs faculty). Lane J1_WAIVER_SIGNAL is over-specific; should be downgraded to a generic visa signal until child page confirms J-1 waiver.

additionalUnresolveds: Cleaned text mentions 'Visiting medical student clerkships Admissions' link but admissions detail (IMG eligibility, fee, duration, VSLO) is not present on this overview.
CRISP (Clinical Research Internship Study Program) is named in a news article but eligibility, application, and campus are not stated.
Pre-College Healthcare Academy is announced for summer 2026 but eligibility, cost, and clinical-vs-non-clinical nature are not stated.
Immigration/visas section does not state whether sponsorship covers visiting students, residents, fellows, or faculty.
